meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: India denounces ‘hellhole’ remark shared by Trump
items.summary: India’s Foreign Ministry says comments by US radio host Michael Savage, circulated by Trump, are 'uninformed'.

items.title: Global hunger report warns of rising malnutrition and famine risks
items.summary: Conflict remains the leading driver of hunger, affecting nearly 150 million people facing acute food shortages.

items.title: Why is there a political deadlock in Lebanon?
items.summary: The Lebanese government wants Hezbollah to disarm as it aims for a state monopoly on weapons.

items.title: Baby twins attend their father’s funeral after Israeli settler attack
items.summary: Palestinians gathered in the occupied West Bank to bury a 25-year-old father of twins shot dead by Israeli settlers.

items.title: Gold is unaffordable so South Asian brides turn to one gram substitutes
items.summary: Families choose imitation jewellery and gold-plated ornaments as record prices push pure gold out of reach.

items.title: US says Iran can play at 2026 World Cup but bars those with ‘IRGC ties’
items.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the US has not told the Iranian national team that it cannot play.

items.title: The story Tehran wants you to read
items.summary: A new narrative about Iran’s leadership mistakes continuity for change - and echoes the regime’s own preferred framing.

items.title: Japan builds up its ‘southern shield’ as faith in US security cover falters
items.summary: Japan is pushing the legal limit of 'defence' as it faces its 'most severe and complex security environment' since 1945.

items.title: UK rights groups slam ‘authoritarian’ conviction of pro-Palestine activists
items.summary: Ruling against Ben Jamal and Chris Nineham will spawn a 'broader chilling effect', groups such as Amnesty and HRW warn.

items.title: Timberwolves take 2-1 NBA playoff lead over Nuggets, Hawks down Knicks
items.summary: Elsewhere, Toronto Raptors beat Cleveland Cavaliers, snapping a 12-game playoff losing streak against the Cavs.

items.title: Ukrainian married couple aged 75 killed in Russian attack on Odesa
items.summary: Ukrainian officials say residential buildings and bulk carrier also hit in overnight assault.

items.title: Does Israel’s ‘Yellow Line’ violate the Lebanon ceasefire?
items.summary: Israel has carved out a military zone inside southern Lebanon, raising fears it is replicating the “Yellow Line” in Gaza

items.title: Trump’s May 1 deadline: Can he continue war on Iran after that?
items.summary: Trump is obliged under the War Powers Act to limit the conflict after 60 days with congressional approval.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 56 after Trump extended ceasefire?
items.summary: The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is extended by three weeks after White House talks with Israeli and Lebanese envoys.

items.title: How fake AI victims are being used to provide rationale for attacking Iran
items.summary: In the battle of propaganda, fake videos and images of female victims of Iran's government are going viral.

items.title: China’s DeepSeek unveils latest models a year after upending global tech
items.summary: Chinese startup says DeepSeek-V4-Pro beats all rival open models for maths and coding.

items.title: Oil rises above $106 per barrel as US, Iran deadlocked in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Jump in prices comes as Donald Trump says vessels will need permission of US Navy to transit key waterway.

items.title: Iran war live: Lebanon truce extended; Trump says time not on Tehran’s side
items.summary: Death toll from Israel's ongoing genocidal war on Gaza has reached 72,568 with 172,338 wounded: Gaza's Health Ministry.

items.title: US professors sue university over arrest during pro-Palestine protest
items.summary: Philosophy professor Noelle McAfee says university failed to protect students and staff in crackdown on protests.

items.title: US soldier charged with using Polymarket to bet on Nicolas Maduro abduction
items.summary: The suspect, 38-year-old Gannon Ken Van Dyke, allegedly made $400,000 from insider knowledge of the Venezuela attack.

items.title: Meta lines up layoffs while Microsoft offers buyouts
items.summary: Meta will lay off 8,000 workers while Microsoft is offering  buyouts to 8,750 people, a first for the Windows maker.

items.title: Milei administration in Argentina blocks journalist access to Casa Rosada
items.summary: Press advocates have warned of hostile rhetoric towards journalists and increasingly restrictive policies under Milei.

items.title: Funeral held for journalist killed in targeted Israeli strike
items.summary: Mourners attended the funeral of Amal Khalil, a journalist who was killed in an Israeli attack in south Lebanon.

items.title: EU formally approves 90bn euro Ukraine loan and new sanctions on Russia
items.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomes the development, urging that the first tranche be disbursed by May or June.

items.title: Pope decries migrants and refugees being treated ‘worse than house pets’
items.summary: Pope Leo has previously criticised Donald Trump's US immigration policies, calling for better treatment of refugees.
